# Book Review

> [!TIP]
> Fill in metadata first. Capture quotes as you read. Complete reflection and rating last. Insert dates with `Ctrl+;`. Press `Alt+A` to archive when done.

---

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Title** | [Book title] |
| **Author** | [Author name] |
| **Genre** | [Genre or category] |
| **Pages** | [Page count] |
| **Date Read** | [Start date - End date] |
| **Rating** | [X / 5] |

## Summary

[Write a 3-5 sentence summary of the book in your own words. What is it about? What is the author's main argument or narrative?]

## Key Ideas

### [First key idea]

[Why does this idea matter? How does it connect to your experience?]

### [Second key idea]

[Your explanation of this idea]

### [Third key idea]

[Your explanation of this idea]

> [!NOTE]
> Limit to 3-5 key ideas. Focus on what changed your thinking, not a chapter-by-chapter recap.

## Favorite Quotes

> "The score takes care of itself."
> — p. 24

> [Another quote that resonated]
> — p. [page number]

## How It Changed My Thinking

[What will you do differently after reading this book?]

## Rating Breakdown

| Criteria | Score (1-5) |
|----------|-------------|
| Writing quality | [X] |
| Practical value | [X] |
| Originality | [X] |
| Enjoyment | [X] |
| **Overall** | **[X]** |
